original_hypothesis.title: Protocol Fee and Referral Fee Permanently Locked in Curves Contract on Sells
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: In _transferFees during sells, the sellValue sent to the seller is computed as price - protocolFee - subjectFee - referralFee - holderFee. The protocolFee is deducted from the seller's payout but never forwarded to protocolFeeDestination (only the sellValue goes to firstDestination which is msg.sender on sells). Similarly, when no referral is defined, the referralFee is deducted from sellValue but the transfer is skipped (returns (true, bytes(''))), permanently locking both amounts in the Curves contract. There is no withdrawal function for this locked ETH.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is an accounting error in the sell fee distribution. ETH accumulates permanently in the Curves contract with no extraction mechanism, representing a continual loss.

proof_of_signal.pattern: For sells: firstDestination=msg.sender, sellValue=price-protocolFee-subjectFee-referralFee-holderFee sent to seller. protocolFee is deducted but never sent anywhere on sells. referralFee skipped if no referral defined.

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: On buy: protocolFee sent to protocolFeeDestination. On sell: protocolFee deducted from seller but never forwarded — asymmetric handling
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: None — happens automatically on every sell

operational_questions: How much ETH has been locked from sells since deployment?
Is there any admin function to extract locked ETH from Curves?
Does this affect contract solvency for future sells?
validation_requirements: Execute a sell, trace ETH flow, verify protocolFee stays in contract
Verify no receive/fallback/withdraw function in Curves for extracting trapped ETH
Calculate cumulative ETH loss over protocol lifetime

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Not directly exploitable for profit, but causes permanent ETH leakage
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Over time, Curves contract accumulates ETH from all sell protocolFees
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Eventually, contract may have insufficient ETH for sell payouts if bonding curve backing erodes
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: No recovery — ETH is permanently locked with no extraction function
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: None — systemic issue on every sell

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Continuous ETH leakage; protocol loses revenue from all sells; potential long-term insolvency
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All sell operations, protocol fee collection

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Curves contract ETH balance growing beyond expected bonding curve reserves
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Protocol fee destination not receiving fees from sells
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Increasing contract balance discrepancy over time

validation_result.mechanism: Asymmetric fee routing locks protocolFee and referralFee on sells
validation_result.impact_summary: Every sell transaction permanently locks the protocolFee (and referralFee when no referral is set) inside the Curves contract with no extraction mechanism, causing continuous ETH leakage from the protocol and sellers.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The _transferFees function handles buy and sell fee distribution asymmetrically. On buys, the firstDestination is protocolFeeDestination and receives protocolFee (plus referralFee if no referral). On sells, firstDestination is msg.sender (the seller), who receives sellValue = price - protocolFee - subjectFee - referralFee - holderFee. The protocolFee is deducted from the seller's payout but is never forwarded to protocolFeeDestination — there is no code path that sends protocolFee to its intended recipient during sells. Similarly, when no referral is defined, the referralFee is deducted from sellValue but the conditional expression evaluates to (true, bytes('')) which sends nothing. Both amounts remain trapped in the Curves contract. There is no withdraw or rescue function for stuck ETH in the contract.

Accounting verification for sells:
- Sent to seller: price - protocolFee - subjectFee - referralFee - holderFee
- Sent to subject: subjectFee
- Sent to referral (if defined): referralFee
- Sent to feeRedistributor: holderFee
- Total sent (referral defined): price - protocolFee
- Total sent (no referral): price - protocolFee - referralFee
- Remaining locked in contract: protocolFee (always) + referralFee (when no referral)
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Lines 196-220 of Curves.sol clearly show the asymmetric handling. The firstDestination on sells is msg.sender, not protocolFeeDestination. The value sent to firstDestination on sells is sellValue (price minus ALL fees), not protocolFee. The protocolFee amount simply remains in the contract. For referralFee, the ternary on line 212-214 skips the transfer entirely when no referral is defined, returning (true, bytes('')).

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: On sells (isBuy=false): firstDestination=msg.sender receives sellValue (price minus ALL fees). protocolFee is deducted from sellValue but never sent to protocolFeeDestination. referralFee is deducted from sellValue but when no referral is defined, the transfer is skipped via (true, bytes('')). Both amounts remain permanently locked in the contract.

calling_sequence: 1. sellCurvesToken validates supply > amount and balance >= amount
2. price = getPrice(supply - amount, amount) computes sell price
4. _transferFees(curvesTokenSubject, false, price, amount, supply) called with isBuy=false
5. getFees(price) computes protocolFee, subjectFee, referralFee, holderFee
7. sellValue = price - protocolFee - subjectFee - referralFee - holderFee
8. msg.sender.call{value: sellValue}('') — seller receives price minus ALL fees
10. If referral defined: referral.call{value: referralFee}(''); if not: (true, bytes('')) — no transfer
12. MISSING: protocolFee never sent to protocolFeeDestination — remains in contract
13. MISSING: referralFee never sent anywhere when no referral — remains in contract
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: In _transferFees at line 207, firstDestination is set to msg.sender for sells. At line 209, sellValue is computed as price minus ALL four fees. At line 210, only sellValue is sent to msg.sender. The protocolFee amount (deducted at line 209) is never transferred to protocolFeeDestination — there is no second transfer for protocolFee on sells. Compare with buys where firstDestination is protocolFeeDestination and receives buyValue (which includes protocolFee). At lines 212-214, when referralDefined is false, the ternary evaluates to (true, bytes('')) which performs no ETH transfer, leaving referralFee locked.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided that would contradict this finding. The bug is inherent in the _transferFees logic.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: This is not an attack per se but a systemic code defect. Every sell transaction where protocolFeePercent > 0 will lock the protocolFee amount in the contract. Every sell transaction where no referralFeeDestination is set for the subject will additionally lock the referralFee amount. Example: If protocolFeePercent is 5% (0.05 ether) and a sell has price = 1 ETH, then 0.05 ETH of protocolFee stays locked in the contract. Over thousands of sells, this accumulates to significant locked ETH. The Curves contract has no receive() fallback, no withdraw() for ETH, and no admin extraction function.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH severity. Every sell permanently locks protocolFee (and referralFee when no referral is set) in the Curves contract. This causes: (1) Protocol loses all fee revenue from sells, (2) Sellers are charged fees that benefit nobody, (3) Locked ETH accumulates over time with no recovery mechanism, (4) Long-term contract solvency could be affected if bonding curve reserves are assumed to match theoretical values. The only fix is contract redeployment or upgrade.
recommended_mitigations: Add an explicit transfer of protocolFee to protocolFeeDestination in the sell path of _transferFees, e.g., add a separate .call to protocolFeeDestination for protocolFee when isBuy is false
When referralDefined is false on sells, either add referralFee to sellValue (returning it to the seller) or send it to protocolFeeDestination
Add an admin rescue function for stuck ETH as a safety net, with appropriate access controls
Fix the Security.sol onlyOwner/onlyManager modifiers to use require() so that access control actually works
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy Curves with non-zero protocolFeePercent. 2. Have subject buy first token. 3. Have another user buy tokens. 4. Have the user sell tokens. 5. Check Curves contract ETH balance — it will have protocolFee ETH more than expected bonding curve reserve. 6. Verify protocolFeeDestination did NOT receive any ETH from the sell. 7. Repeat without setting referralFeeDestination and verify referralFee is also locked.
uncertainties: The exact cumulative impact depends on trade volume and fee percentages configured at deployment
additional_testing_needed: Quantitative analysis of ETH locked per sell at various fee percentages
Verify that locked ETH eventually causes insolvency when many users try to sell simultaneously
Test interaction with the holdersFeePercent path when feeRedistributor is set vs not set
